title: Anesthesia Equipment MCQ – Section 2
source: Short Textbook of Anesthesia – Ajay Yadav

questions.question: In an anesthesia machine, the high-pressure system delivers oxygen to the intermediate pressure system at what pressure?
questions.explanation: The 1st stage (primary) pressure regulator reduces cylinder pressure to 45–60 psi, which is then further reduced by the 2nd stage regulator to 15–35 psi for the intermediate system.

questions.question: Which valve prevents delivery of a hypoxic gas mixture by cutting off or proportionately reducing nitrous oxide flow when oxygen pressure falls?
questions.explanation: The fail-safe valve (oxygen supply failure protection device) proportionately decreases N₂O flow when oxygen pressure drops, preventing a hypoxic mixture.

questions.question: What is the color code for the nitrous oxide flow control knob on an anesthesia machine rotameter?
questions.explanation: Color codes: Blue = N₂O, White = O₂, Black = Air.

questions.question: In a rotameter, the oxygen flowmeter tube should be positioned:
questions.explanation: Oxygen tube must be the most downstream so that at the common manifold junction, leakage affects O₂ at only 2 positions, minimising hypoxic mixture risk.

questions.question: What minimum FiO₂ does the oxygen-nitrous proportioning system guarantee to prevent hypoxia?
questions.explanation: The O₂–N₂O interconnected pulley system ensures a minimum FiO₂ of 25%, making singular N₂O flow impossible.

questions.question: The flowmeter tubes in a rotameter are also called:
questions.explanation: Glass variable-orifice flowmeter tubes are called Thorpe's tubes; they are tapered (narrow at base, wide at apex).

questions.question: Which vaporizer is specifically designed for desflurane delivery?
questions.explanation: Tec 6 is a heated and pressurised vaporizer specifically designed for desflurane due to its very low boiling point (23.5°C) and high vapour pressure.

questions.question: The Aladin cassette vaporizer is notable because:

options.B: It can deliver all inhalational agents with color-coded cassettes

questions.explanation: The Aladin cassette vaporizer is the latest design; insertion of a color-coded agent cassette automatically adjusts all settings.

questions.question: What is the color of the dial knob on a desflurane vaporizer?
questions.explanation: Vaporizer color codes: Red = halothane, Purple = isoflurane, Yellow = sevoflurane, Blue = desflurane.

questions.question: When two vaporizers are mounted on the same machine, the agent with the LOWER boiling point should be placed:
questions.explanation: Agent with lower boiling point placed first (nearest rotameter); otherwise condensed particles from the first vaporizer contaminate the second.

questions.question: The maximum permissible concentration of nitrous oxide in the operating theater is:
questions.explanation: Maximum permissible: N₂O = 25 ppm; halogenated agents < 2 ppm. The scavenging system eliminates exhaled gases to achieve this.

questions.question: In the Mapleson classification, which circuit is the gold standard for SPONTANEOUS ventilation?
questions.explanation: Magill (Type A) is the circuit of choice for spontaneous ventilation. Fresh gas flow = minute volume prevents rebreathing.

questions.question: The Bain circuit is a modification of which Mapleson type?
questions.explanation: Bain incorporated an inner tube into Mapleson D; fresh gases travel through the inner tube minimising mixing with exhaled gases.

questions.question: The recommended fresh gas flow for the Bain circuit during controlled ventilation at normal respiratory rates is:
questions.explanation: Bain circuit controlled ventilation: 1.6 × MV at 10–12 breaths/min, or 70–100 mL/kg/min if RR increased to 16/min.

questions.question: For the Magill circuit used during controlled ventilation, the required fresh gas flow is approximately:
questions.explanation: Magill requires > 3 × minute volume for controlled ventilation and is still unreliable; hence it should NOT be used for controlled ventilation.

questions.question: The Jackson-Rees circuit (Mapleson F) is most commonly used for:
questions.explanation: Jackson-Rees (Mapleson F) is the most commonly used pediatric semiclosed circuit for children < 6 years or < 20 kg.

questions.question: The APL valve is also known as:
questions.explanation: APL = Adjustable Pressure Limiting valve, also called expiratory valve and rebreathing prevention valve. Most common type: Heidbrink.

questions.question: The reservoir bag capacity for adults in an anesthesia breathing circuit is:
questions.explanation: Reservoir bag sizes: Neonates 250 mL, children ≤3 yrs 500 mL, children >3 yrs 1000 mL, adults 2000 mL.

questions.question: Who first introduced the closed circuit (circle system) in humans?
questions.explanation: Waters introduced the closed circuit (circle system) in humans in 1923.

questions.question: The standard composition of sodalime includes all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Ba(OH)₂ is a component of barylime, NOT sodalime. Sodalime: Ca(OH)₂ 94%, NaOH 5% (catalyst), KOH 1%, color indicator, silica.

questions.question: Which CO₂ absorbent has been withdrawn from market due to risk of fire and explosion with sevoflurane?
questions.explanation: Barylime was withdrawn because experiments showed absorber temperatures reaching > 200°C with sevoflurane, causing fire/explosion risk.

questions.question: Compound A, a nephrotoxic product, is produced by the reaction of sevoflurane with:
questions.explanation: Sevoflurane reacts with sodalime and barylime to produce compound A (nephrotoxic). Risk is higher with barylime vs sodalime.

questions.question: Desiccated sodalime produces carbon monoxide most significantly with which agent?
questions.explanation: CO production with desiccated sodalime in decreasing order: Desflurane >> Isoflurane >> Halothane = Sevoflurane.

questions.question: Which inhalational agent, when reacting with sodalime, can produce dichloroacetylene (neurotoxic) and phosgene (causes ARDS)?
questions.explanation: Trielene (trichloroethylene) reacts with sodalime to produce dichloroacetylene (neurotoxic) and phosgene gas (causes ARDS).

questions.question: The color indicator Durasorb used in India indicates fresh sodalime as:

options.A: White when fresh, purple when exhausted
options.B: Pink when fresh, white when exhausted
options.C: Blue when fresh, white when exhausted
options.D: White when fresh, pink when exhausted

questions.explanation: Durasorb (most common in India): pink when fresh → white when exhausted. Ethyl violet: white when fresh → purple when exhausted.

questions.question: One hundred grams of sodalime can absorb approximately how much carbon dioxide?
questions.explanation: 100 g of sodalime absorbs 24–26 L of CO₂. Granule size is 4–8 mesh (3–6 mm), moisture content 14–19%, airspace > 50%.

questions.question: Amsorb (calcium hydroxide lime) has the following advantage over sodalime:

options.C: Does not produce compound A or carbon monoxide

questions.explanation: Amsorb produces neither compound A nor CO. Disadvantages: high cost and absorptive capacity approximately half of sodalime.

questions.question: In the closed circuit, the oxygen analyzer is fitted in:
questions.explanation: Oxygen analyzer is fitted in the inspiratory limb to measure the final delivered O₂ concentration to the patient.

questions.question: The negative pressure leak test (universal leak test) of the anesthesia machine can detect leaks as small as:
questions.explanation: The negative pressure (suction bulb) test is the most sensitive, detecting leaks as small as 30 mL. It is called the universal leak test.

questions.question: When performing the breathing circuit check, the system is flushed to attain a pressure of:
questions.explanation: Close APL valve, occlude Y-piece, flush O₂ to 30 cm H₂O; if maintained for > 10 seconds, no leak is confirmed.

questions.question: Which of the following airway equipment was introduced by Karl Storz and is characterised by a fiberoptic bundle in the blade?
questions.explanation: Bullard laryngoscope is an indirect rigid fiberoptic laryngoscope useful for difficult airways. Macintosh and Miller are standard direct laryngoscopes.

questions.question: The Laryngeal Mask Airway (LMA) is classified as which type of airway device?
questions.explanation: LMA is a supraglottic airway device; it sits above the glottis in the hypopharynx. Endotracheal tube is an infraglottic device.

questions.question: The ProSeal LMA differs from classic LMA in that it:

options.B: Has a drain tube allowing passage of a gastric tube
options.C: Can only be used in children

questions.explanation: ProSeal LMA has an integral drain tube allowing gastric tube insertion and provides a better glottic seal than classic LMA.

questions.question: The I-gel airway device is unique because:

options.B: It has a non-inflatable gel cuff that conforms to airway anatomy
options.C: It is used only in pediatric patients
options.D: It is a nasal airway device

questions.explanation: I-gel has a non-inflatable gel cuff (thermoplastic elastomer) that anatomically conforms to the supraglottic structure, eliminating the need for cuff inflation.

questions.question: An oropharyngeal (Guedel) airway should NOT be used in:

options.D: Patients with a gag reflex absent

questions.explanation: Guedel airway stimulates the gag reflex and may cause vomiting, laryngospasm, or bronchospasm in semi-conscious or conscious patients.

questions.question: A nasopharyngeal airway is preferred over an oropharyngeal airway in which clinical situation?

options.B: Patients with intact gag reflex or trismus (limited mouth opening)

questions.explanation: Nasopharyngeal airways are better tolerated in semi-conscious patients with intact gag reflex and in patients with limited mouth opening (trismus).

questions.question: The AMBU bag (self-inflating resuscitator) can deliver oxygen concentrations up to what percentage when connected to an O₂ source with a reservoir bag?
questions.explanation: With an O₂ source and attached reservoir bag, the AMBU bag can deliver close to 100% O₂.

questions.question: A Venturi mask delivers a fixed FiO₂ because:

options.A: It uses a reservoir bag to store O₂
options.B: It uses the Bernoulli/Venturi principle to entrain a fixed ratio of room air
options.C: It is a high-flow device that delivers 100% O₂
options.D: It monitors O₂ via an internal sensor

questions.explanation: The Venturi mask uses the Venturi/Bernoulli principle: high-velocity O₂ jet entrains a fixed ratio of room air, providing a precise FiO₂.

questions.question: The Macintosh laryngoscope blade tip is placed in:

options.C: Directly under the epiglottis (posterior to epiglottis)

questions.explanation: Macintosh (curved) blade tip sits in the vallecula; lifting stretches the glossoepiglottic ligament to lift the epiglottis indirectly. Miller (straight) blade goes posterior to epiglottis.

questions.question: Humidification of inspired gases is important because dry anesthetic gases can cause all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Dry gases cause impaired mucociliary clearance, thick secretions, atelectasis, and hypothermia. They do NOT cause increased CO₂ production.

questions.question: Static electricity in the operating room is a hazard because it can cause:
questions.explanation: Static current can ignite explosive anesthetic gases. Equipment is made antistatic to prevent this; e.g., antistatic rotameter bobbins.

questions.question: Sterilization of anesthesia equipment by autoclaving (steam under pressure) is MOST suitable for:
questions.explanation: Autoclaving is suitable for heat-stable metal instruments. Rubber and plastic components may be damaged by heat; use chemical/cold sterilization instead.

questions.question: In an ascending bellows ventilator, why are leaks better detected compared to descending bellows?

options.A: Ascending bellows makes more noise when leaking
options.B: Descending bellows will continue to descend under gravity even with a leak; ascending bellows will fail to ascend
options.C: Ascending bellows has a higher compliance
options.D: It has nothing to do with gravity

questions.explanation: Descending bellows fall under gravity even with a leak (masking the leak), while ascending bellows fail to ascend if there is a leak, making the problem obvious.

questions.question: Fresh gas flow compensation in modern anesthesia workstation ventilators primarily serves to:

options.B: Maintain stable preset tidal volume despite fresh gas flow leakage variations
options.C: Ensure N₂O does not exceed 25 ppm

questions.explanation: Fresh gas flow compensation and fresh gas decoupling maintain stable delivery of preset tidal volume by compensating for fresh gas flow variations.

questions.question: Which factor does NOT increase CO₂ absorption in the circle (closed) circuit?
questions.explanation: Too tightly packed sodalime decreases airspace (< 50%), reducing gas contact time and CO₂ absorption. All other options enhance absorption.

questions.question: Channeling in a sodalime canister refers to:

options.B: Gas flowing through gaps between granules and canister wall, bypassing absorption
options.C: Color change of the indicator dye

questions.explanation: Channeling: too loosely packed sodalime allows gas to pass through gaps between granules and canister wall without CO₂ being absorbed.

questions.question: A Lack system is a coaxial modification of which Mapleson circuit?
questions.explanation: Lack system is the coaxial version of Mapleson A (Magill). It has two concentric tubes: outer = inspiratory, inner = expiratory.

questions.question: For the oxygen analyzer in a closed circuit, what reading confirms it is functioning correctly when exposed to air?
questions.explanation: The O₂ analyzer should read 21% when exposed to air (atmospheric O₂ concentration) and > 90% when connected to the O₂-flushed system.

questions.question: Among semiclosed circuits, the order of efficiency for CONTROLLED ventilation (best to worst) is:
questions.explanation: For controlled ventilation: DEF > BC > A. For spontaneous ventilation: A > DEF > BC. Magill (A) is worst for controlled ventilation.

questions.question: The Pin Index Safety System (PISS) on cylinder yokes prevents:

options.B: Wrong gas cylinders from being connected to the wrong yokes

questions.explanation: PISS uses specific hole positions on cylinder valves corresponding to pins on the machine yoke, ensuring only the correct gas cylinder can be connected.
